shortAnswer: Folate (folic acid) is a B vitamin essential for cell division and blood formation. A deficiency can lead to anemia and is particularly dangerous during pregnancy. Many people do not get enough folate through diet alone. Discuss any deficiency with your doctor.
metaDescription: Folate too low? Normal ranges, pregnancy risks, causes of folic acid deficiency: explained simply.
doctorQuestions: Should I take folate during or before pregnancy?
Is my folate level sufficient for normal blood formation?
Which foods are rich in folate?
pageTitle: Folate value: Folic Acid explained | Medyra
